case_name: Palmer v. Shawnee Mission Med. Ctr., Inc.

summary.brief: Palmer v. Shawnee Mission Med. Ctr., Inc.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Ms. Palmer's husband testified that he bases his intentional infliction of emotional distress claim on the misdiagnosis of false labor by the medical providers who treated Ms. Palmer.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court grants summary judgment against the intentional infliction of emotional distress claim asserted against defendant MAPS.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The court grants summary judgment against plaintiffs' intentional infliction of emotional distress claim asserted against both defendants SMMC and MAPS because the summary judgment facts-viewed in plaintiffs' favor-present no triable issue whether defendants engaged in extreme and outrageous conduct.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Viewing the summary judgment facts in plaintiffs' favor, they establish that SMMC staff diagnosed Ms. Palmer as having false labor and discharged her from the hospital. Less than two hours later, Ms. Palmer delivered her baby at home.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court also grants summary judgment against plaintiffs' intentional infliction of emotional distress claim for another, independent reason. Plaintiffs have failed to adduce any evidence that would permit a reasonable jury to find that any of the four plaintiffs sustained extreme and severe mental distress.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Ms. Palmer concedes that she sustained no physical injuries whatsoever from giving birth to her son at home on November 5, 2014.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Palmer testified that her son is normal and healthy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Palmer has not sought any medical or psychological treatment as a result of the events of November 5, 2014.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court thus grants summary judgment against plaintiffs' intentional infliction of emotional distress claim for this second, independent reason.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The court denies plaintiffs' summary judgment motion seeking judgment in their favor on their intentional infliction of emotional distress claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Cramps, vaginal bleeding, and pinkish fluid discharge
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Ms. Palmer, 36.2 weeks pregnant, presented to SMMC's Birth Center with cramping, vaginal bleeding, and pinkish fluid discharge.

focused_exam.obstetric: Cervix dilated to fingertip width, 100% effaced, baby's head at -2 station

requestables.name: Fern and Nitrazine tests
requestables.description: Tests to check for the presence of amniotic fluid

reveal.decision_impact: Contributed to diagnosis of false labor

requestables.name: Ultrasound
requestables.description: Ultrasound to assess fluid levels around the baby

reveal.clinical_significance: Could have provided additional information on fetal status

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to SMMC's Birth Center with symptoms

key_events.event: Patient admitted for observation and assessment

timeline_phases.name: Assessment and Monitoring
timeline_phases.description: Patient monitored and assessed for labor

key_events.event: Vital signs and vaginal exams performed

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in Birth Center with symptoms suggestive of labor
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Perform an ultrasound to assess fetal status
options.clinical_reasoning: Ultrasound could provide crucial information on fetal well-being and fluid levels

options.description: Rely on Fern and Nitrazine tests and discharge if negative
options.clinical_reasoning: Tests were deemed sufficient by staff, but may not fully assess labor status

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant relied on Fern and Nitrazine tests and discharged patient
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Tests indicated no rupture of membranes

expected_action_court.description: An ultrasound should have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Would provide a more comprehensive assessment of labor status

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to perform ultrasound may have led to misdiagnosis of labor status
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Ultrasound should have been used to confirm labor status
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care requires comprehensive assessment in suspected labor

patient_outcome.description: Patient delivered baby at home less than two hours after discharge

key_findings: No expert testimony to establish deviation from standard of care
No evidence of extreme and outrageous conduct

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Admitting for observation could have prevented home delivery

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to SMMC's Birth Center
Patient presents with symptoms suggestive of preterm labor
Patient discharged with diagnosis of false labor

allegations.allegation: Failure to provide appropriate medical screening

court_findings.legal_significance: No liability for intentional infliction of emotional distress
